name: jsbeautifier
documentation: Beautify, unpack or deobfuscate JavaScript. Handles popular online obfuscators.

modules.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.evalbased
documentation:
Unpacker for eval() based packers: runs JS code and returns result.
Works only if a JS interpreter (e.g. Mozilla's Rhino) is installed and
properly set up on host.
Unpacker for eval() based packers, a part of javascript beautifier
by Einar Lielmanis <einar@beautifier.io>
written by Stefano Sanfilippo <a.little.coder@gmail.com>
usage:
if detect(some_string):
unpacked = unpack(some_string)

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.evalbased.detect
documentation: Detects if source is likely to be eval() packed.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.evalbased.unpack
documentation: Runs source and return resulting code.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.evalbased.jseval
documentation: Run code in the JS interpreter and return output.
In case of failure, we'll just return the original, without crashing on user.

modules.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.myobfuscate
documentation: Deobfuscator for scripts messed up with MyObfuscate.com
deobfuscator for scripts messed up with myobfuscate.com
by Einar Lielmanis <einar@beautifier.io>
written by Stefano Sanfilippo <a.little.coder@gmail.com>
usage:
if detect(some_string):
unpacked = unpack(some_string)
CAVEAT by Einar Lielmanis
You really don't want to obfuscate your scripts there: they're tracking
your unpackings, your script gets turned into something like this,
var _escape = 'your_script_escaped';
var _111 = document.createElement('script');
_111.src = 'http://api.www.myobfuscate.com/?getsrc=ok' +
'&ref=' + encodeURIComponent(document.referrer) +
'&url=' + encodeURIComponent(document.URL);
var 000 = document.getElementsByTagName('head')[0];
000.appendChild(_111);
document.write(unescape(_escape));

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.myobfuscate.detect
documentation: Detects MyObfuscate.com packer.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.myobfuscate.unpack
documentation: Unpacks js code packed with MyObfuscate.com

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.myobfuscate._filter
documentation: Extracts and decode payload (original file) from `source`

modules.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.javascriptobfuscator
documentation: deobfuscator for scripts messed up with JavascriptObfuscator.com
simple unpacker/deobfuscator for scripts messed up with
javascriptobfuscator.com
written by Einar Lielmanis <einar@beautifier.io>
rewritten in Python by Stefano Sanfilippo <a.little.coder@gmail.com>
Will always return valid javascript: if `detect()` is false, `code` is
returned, unmodified.
usage:
if javascriptobfuscator.detect(some_string):
some_string = javascriptobfuscator.unpack(some_string)

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.javascriptobfuscator.smartsplit
documentation: Split `code` at " symbol, only if it is not escaped.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.javascriptobfuscator.detect
documentation: Detects if `code` is JavascriptObfuscator.com packed.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.javascriptobfuscator.unpack
documentation: Unpacks JavascriptObfuscator.com packed code.

modules.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers
documentation: General code for JSBeautifier unpackers infrastructure.
General code for JSBeautifier unpackers infrastructure. See README.specs
written by Stefano Sanfilippo <a.little.coder@gmail.com>

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.getunpackers
documentation:
Scans the unpackers dir, finds unpackers and add them to UNPACKERS list.
An unpacker will be loaded only if it is a valid python module (name must
adhere to naming conventions) and it is not blacklisted (i.e. inserted
into BLACKLIST.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.run
documentation: Runs the applicable unpackers and return unpacked source as a string.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.filtercomments
documentation: NOT USED: strips trailing comments and put them at the top.

classes.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.UnpackingError
documentation:
Badly packed source or general error. Argument is a
meaningful description.

modules.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.urlencode
documentation: Bookmarklet/escaped script unpacker.
Trivial bookmarklet/escaped script detector for the javascript beautifier
written by Einar Lielmanis <einar@beautifier.io>
rewritten in Python by Stefano Sanfilippo <a.little.coder@gmail.com>
Will always return valid javascript: if `detect()` is false, `code` is
returned, unmodified.
usage:
some_string = urlencode.unpack(some_string)

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.urlencode.detect
documentation: Detects if a scriptlet is urlencoded.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.unpackers.urlencode.unpack
documentation: URL decode `code` source string.

functions.name: jsbeautifier.core.options._mergeOpts
documentation: merges child options up with the parent options object
Example: obj = {a: 1, b: {a: 2}}
mergeOpts(obj, 'b')
Returns: {a: 2}
